title: Tour de France stage 18 preview: back to Orcieres-Merlette, where Wout van Aert discovered himself as a climber

news.title: Tour de France stage 18 preview: back to Orcières-Merlette, where Wout van Aert discovered himself as a climber

world.title: Tour de France stage 18 preview: back to Orcieres-Merlette, where Wout van Aert discovered himself as a climber

environment.compositionIntent: A warm-earth, teal-counterpoint Pollock surround keeps the work all-over and decentralized while leaving room for the accepted middleground Tour stage pressure band and the foreground thrown mark field to read as separate systems.

distanceSafety.reason: Canonical public camera retained for attempt 1 because all accepted isolated previews are broad horizontal all-over fields and no evidence currently requires portrait-specific reframing.

contrast.notes: Warm key light keeps the stained ground open; restrained teal rim separates cool counterfields and black-white skeins without turning the scene into a generic glow effect.

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: background atmosphere, palette/value field, shallow depth, and active negative space
surrounding WebGPU-safe environment behavior that gives other parts room to read
avoid: main news-pressure band unless explicitly assigned
main knot or focal tangle unless explicitly assigned

compositionAdaptations: Kept at the particle and skybox-safe origin as the accepted background shell.
World radius and warm-earth color grade preserve the isolated preview's pale ground, black-white skeins, teal stains, and active negative space without assigning it the main news-pressure band.
Kept at origin as the background shell, with warm-earth/teal world grade preserving active negative space.
composition modified world-environment from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Session Playwright preview attempt 3 rendered successfully with healthStatus ok and captureStatus ok.
Desktop screenshot shows a dominant warm-earth Pollock environment with black/white poured skeins, teal/ochre stained atmosphere, scattered chromatic specks, and no literal cycling subject.

Render report confirms WebGPU backend and WebGPU-safe surfaces: MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, sky-background-field, color-field, procedural-marks, soft-particles, and utilities.createDirectionSpaceSkyboxShell evidence.

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form

compositionAdaptations: Held in the declared middleground role as the dominant low-pressure measured diagonal climb band.
Scaled large enough to keep the black contour, white skein, ochre stage cue, teal counterflow, and compressed gate slabs readable against the all-over environment.
Held as the dominant middleground diagonal pressure band for the measured Tour stage climb cue.
composition modified non-literal-flow-pressure from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the local concept reference image directly as advisory-only palette/density evidence: warm earth ground, teal counterfield, black/white skeins, ochre/yellow/orange/red/blue accents, dense but decentralized Pollock-like pressure.
Static check passed with validation.pass true and no errors for drafts/non-literal-flow-pressure.module.mjs.
Playwright preview attempt 1 succeeded and captured desktop screenshot previews/attempt-001/001-non-literal-flow-pressure-draft-desktop.jpg.

Desktop screenshot showed a nonblank, camera-safe, large middleground diagonal pressure structure: warm-earth and teal fields, compressed gate slabs, black/white/ochre/teal tube paths, and controlled pigment drops expressing the Tour stage climb as low-pressure measured flow.

Render report previews/attempt-001/desktop-render-report.json reported healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, WebGPU renderer/backend, and WebGPU-safe material evidence for MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, color-field, procedural-marks, and soft-particles.

owns: middleground selected-news pressure structure: dominant band, grid compression, barricade, or equivalent pressure form
avoid: full foreground artist mark field of loops, scratches, drips, splatters, and near-line behavior

paramSemantics.band: visual placement/readability band for full-world params; distinct from declared compositionOwnership.lane

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionAdaptations: Kept in the declared foreground role as near artist mark behavior: thrown loops, scratches, drips, splatter, dot-grid catches, and chromatic accents.
Offset slightly right and reduced relative to the primary band so the foreground marks intensify the Pollock surface without becoming a second middleground news-pressure structure.
Kept as foreground Pollock loops, drips, splatter, and chromatic catches, offset and scaled so it supports rather than replaces the pressure band.
composition modified artist-mark-field from isolated accepted-part defaults
workshopAcceptance: Inspected the advisory concept reference image directly for broad palette, density, and all-over Pollock mark mood.
Static check passed for drafts/artist-mark-field.module.mjs with sourceHash a06ed401dacba7b233ac9857309a265cb8f569e3cb1f5fbec5cb6d21d7d14a38.

Attempt 2 Playwright desktop preview rendered a nonblank, readable foreground Pollock mark field with large white and black thrown loops, drips, splatter, ochre/rust/teal/blue accents, and a kinetic lower-left to upper-right Tour stage pressure path.

Attempt 2 render report healthStatus ok, captureStatus ok, rendererMode webgpu, rendererBackend webgpu, with WebGPU-safe MeshBasicMaterial, LineBasicMaterial, PointsMaterial, and runtime material factory evidence for procedural-marks, color-field, and soft-particles.

owns: foreground artist mark and line behavior: loops, scratches, drips, splatters, dot/grid catches, and near readable marks
avoid: another dominant middleground news-pressure band, barricade/grid, or knot structure

compositionEvidence.draftIntent: Preserve the three accepted WebGPU-compatible Pollock modules and assemble them into an all-over warm-earth/teal immersive field where Orcières-Merlette stage 18 reads as a low-pressure kinetic diagonal: environment as surrounding field, flow-pressure as middleground climb structure, artist-mark-field as foreground action-painting skein and splatter system.
